page: Amazon Web Services r8gn.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server instance powered by AWS Graviton processors. It features 96 physical cores on an arm64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. With a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio, the system provides 96 dedicated vCPUs, ensuring no resource sharing at the processor level.

The instance is equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per physical core. It does not include local NVMe storage, meaning all storage operations rely on external Amazon Elastic Block Store (EBS) volumes.

For connectivity, the server provides a baseline network bandwidth of 300 Gbps, optimized for high-throughput networking and EBS performance. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the Graviton-based arm64 architecture delivers a high density of memory and compute resources per unit of power, offering a favorable qualitative cost-to-performance ratio for memory-bound workloads compared to traditional x86 architectures.

This server configuration is designed for memory-intensive workloads, large-scale databases, in-memory caches, real-time big data analytics, and high-throughput network applications that require dedicated physical cores without hyperthreading overhead.
description: Amazon Web Services r8gn.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server instance powered by 96-core AWS Graviton processors on an arm64 architecture. Operating at 2.8 GHz with dedicated physical cores and no hyperthreading, it delivers 768.0 GB of system memory, translating to 8.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance features a baseline network bandwidth of 300 Gbps and is optimized for external EBS storage, as it contains no local storage. The arm64 architecture provides high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive applications. The dedicated core allocation ensures predictable performance without resource contention, making it highly reliable for enterprise operations, large-scale databases, and in-memory caching.
